hero.badge: Free federal filing for simple returns
hero.heading: File your taxes in minutes — not hours
hero.subheading: Taxly guides you step by step through your federal and state return in plain English. No jargon. No surprise fees. Maximum refund.
hero.noCard: No credit card required · Free federal return

howItWorks.heading: Three steps. You stay in control.
howItWorks.subheading: We do the math. You make the decisions.

step1.title: Import your docs
step1.description: Upload your W-2, 1099s, or connect directly to your employer. Taxly pulls in your numbers automatically.

step2.title: Answer plain questions
step2.description: No tax forms, no jargon. We ask simple questions and put your answers in the right boxes behind the scenes.

step3.title: Review & e-file
step3.description: See your refund or balance due before you file. E-file in one click — the IRS typically processes in 21 days.

whatYouNeed.heading: What you'll need
items: W-2 from your employer (or 1099 if self-employed)
Social Security numbers for you, your spouse, and dependents
Last year's tax return (optional — speeds things up)
Bank account details for direct deposit
1099-INT or 1099-DIV if you have investment income
Receipts for deductions you plan to claim

imports.heading: Import from anywhere
imports.description: Connect your employer, brokerage, or bank. Or upload a PDF from TurboTax, H&R Block, or last year's Taxly return.

situations.heading: It covers your situation
situations.subheading: Whether you're a W-2 employee or running a side hustle, Taxly has you covered.

employee.title: W-2 Employee
employee.description: Simple filing for salaried or hourly workers. File federal free.

freelancer.title: Freelancer & Gig Worker
freelancer.description: 1099-NEC, Schedule C, self-employment tax, quarterly estimates — all handled.

investor.title: Investor
investor.description: Stocks, crypto, dividends, capital gains. Import from your brokerage automatically.

homeowner.title: Homeowner
homeowner.description: Mortgage interest, property tax, home-office deduction — we find every dollar.

family.title: Family & Dependents
family.description: Child Tax Credit, Child & Dependent Care, education credits, and more.

retirement.title: Retirement Income
retirement.description: Social Security, 401(k), IRA distributions, pension income — filed correctly.

security.heading: Your tax data is safe with us
security.subheading: We handle your most sensitive financial information. Here's exactly how we protect it.

encryption.title: AES-256 Encryption at Rest
encryption.description: Every piece of data you enter — your SSN, income, deductions — is encrypted with AES-256 before it ever touches our database. The same standard used by banks and the US government.

transit.title: TLS 1.3 in Transit
transit.description: All data moving between your browser and our servers is protected by TLS 1.3. Your information is never sent in plain text, ever.

mfa.title: Multi-Factor Authentication
mfa.description: Protect your account with a second factor — authenticator app or SMS. We recommend enabling MFA before you start filing.

noSell.title: We Never Sell Your Data
noSell.description: Your tax information is yours. We don't sell it, share it with advertisers, or use it to build profiles. We use it for one thing: filing your taxes.

retention.title: Data Retention & Deletion
retention.description: We retain your filed returns for 7 years so you can access them anytime. You can request full deletion of your account and data at any time.

access.title: Strict Access Controls
access.description: Taxly employees cannot access your tax data without your explicit permission. All internal access is logged and audited.

security.certNote: We are actively pursuing SOC 2 Type II certification. Our infrastructure follows its controls today.

guaranteesPage.heading: Three promises we actually keep
guaranteesPage.subheading: No fine print. No asterisks. Here's exactly what each guarantee means and what you get.

maxRefund.title: Maximum Refund Guarantee
maxRefund.whatText: If you file the same return with another major tax software (TurboTax, H&R Block, FreeTaxUSA) and they produce a larger federal refund using identical data, we'll refund your Taxly filing fee in full.
maxRefund.howText: File with Taxly first. Then file with the other software. If their refund is higher, email us at hello@gettaxly.com within 60 days of your Taxly e-file date with both return summaries. We'll verify and refund within 5 business days.
maxRefund.limits: This guarantee covers federal returns only and requires that the same deductions and income are entered in both returns.

accuracy.title: 100% Accuracy Guarantee
accuracy.whatText: If the IRS or a state tax authority assesses a penalty or interest charge that results directly from a Taxly calculation error — not from information you entered incorrectly — we'll pay that penalty and interest.
accuracy.howText: Contact us at hello@gettaxly.com with your IRS notice. We'll review whether the error originated in Taxly's calculations. If it did, we cover the penalty and interest charges. No arguments, no delays.
accuracy.limits: This does not cover penalties from incorrect information you entered, late filing, or situations where you chose to override Taxly's recommendations.

audit.title: Audit Support
audit.whatText: If the IRS or a state tax authority contacts you about a return you filed with Taxly, we'll provide guidance at every step of your response — what the notice means, what documents to gather, and what to say.
audit.howText: Forward the IRS or state notice to support@gettaxly.com. A tax specialist will review it and get back to you within one business day with a plain-English explanation and next steps.
audit.limits: Audit support means guidance and assistance, not legal representation. For full representation before the IRS, consult a licensed CPA or tax attorney.

maxRefund.title: Maximum Refund Guarantee
maxRefund.description: If another tax software gets you a larger refund using the same data, we'll refund your filing fee.

accuracy.title: 100% Accuracy Guarantee
accuracy.description: If Taxly makes a calculation error that costs you a penalty, we pay the penalty plus interest.

audit.title: Audit Support
audit.description: If the IRS contacts you about a return filed with Taxly, we'll walk you through every step of the response.

pricing.heading: One honest price. No surprises at checkout.
pricing.subheading: The number you see is the number you pay.

free.name: Free
free.description: Simple W-2 returns. No income limits.

q1.question: Is the Free tier really free — no hidden fees?
q1.answer: Yes. Free means $0 for federal and $0 for one state return. We don't ask for your credit card until you upgrade to Deluxe or Premium. The price you see when you start is the price you pay.

q2.question: What does 'per state' mean?
q2.answer: Each state return costs $29, regardless of which plan you're on (Deluxe or Premium). So two states = $58, three states = $87. There are no state fees on the Free plan for your first state.

q3.question: Can I upgrade after I start?
q3.answer: Yes. Start on Free and upgrade to Deluxe or Premium any time before you file. Your data carries over and you only pay when you e-file.

q4.question: Do prices change next year?
q4.answer: We lock your price for the tax year you file. If you start a 2025 return at $39, that's what you pay — even if we raise prices in 2026.

q5.question: Is there a refund policy?
q5.answer: If you paid and have not yet e-filed, contact us within 30 days and we'll refund you. Once a return is e-filed and accepted by the IRS, we cannot refund the filing fee.

honestMaths.heading: The honest maths: three ways to file
honestMaths.intro: Including the two that don't involve paying us. If one of these fits, use it — sending you to software you don't need is the exact behaviour this product exists to reject.

directFile.title: IRS Direct File
directFile.text: Free and official. If your income is all W-2, you take the standard deduction, and your state participates — this is simply the right choice.

freetaxusa.title: FreeTaxUSA
freetaxusa.text: The honest budget option — genuinely. If you know your tax situation and a form-like interface doesn't slow you down, it's the cheapest credible way to file.

taxly.title: Taxly Premium
taxly.text: What the premium buys: a plain-English guided interview that finds deductions a first-time 1099 filer doesn't know exist — at a price that can't change at checkout.

featuresPage.heading: Every situation, covered
featuresPage.subheading: Taxly supports the tax situations that matter most for US individuals and families.

help.heading: Help Centre
help.subheading: Quick answers to the most common questions.

q1.question: Do I need to create an account to start?
q1.answer: Yes, a free account is required to save your progress and file your return. It takes under a minute to sign up — just an email and password.

q2.question: Which tax year can I file with Taxly?
q2.answer: You can file the current tax year return (due April 15) and amend returns from the prior two years.

q3.question: Can I import my return from another software?
q3.answer: Yes. Upload a PDF of your prior-year TurboTax, H&R Block, or Taxly return and we'll pre-fill your basic info and prior-year figures.

q4.question: Can I use Taxly on mobile?
q4.answer: Yes. Taxly is fully responsive and works on any smartphone or tablet browser. A dedicated mobile app is planned.

q1.question: When do I pay?
q1.answer: You only pay when you e-file your return. You can prepare your return and see your refund estimate completely free before deciding.

q2.question: What payment methods do you accept?
q2.answer: We accept all major credit and debit cards (Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Discover) and Apple Pay / Google Pay.

q3.question: Can I get a refund?
q3.answer: If you've paid but not yet e-filed, contact us within 30 days for a full refund. After e-filing, the fee is non-refundable.

q1.question: Is my Social Security number safe?
q1.answer: Yes. Your SSN is encrypted with AES-256 before storage and is never transmitted in plain text. It's only sent to the IRS during e-filing, over an encrypted connection.

q2.question: Does Taxly sell my data?
q2.answer: Never. We use your data solely to prepare and file your taxes. We do not sell it, share it with advertisers, or use it to build advertising profiles.

q3.question: How do I enable two-factor authentication?
q3.answer: Go to Account Settings → Security → Enable 2FA. We support authenticator apps (Google Authenticator, Authy) and SMS.

q1.question: How long does IRS processing take?
q1.answer: The IRS typically accepts e-filed returns within 24–48 hours and issues refunds within 21 days of acceptance. You can check status at IRS.gov/refunds.

q2.question: What if I make a mistake after filing?
q2.answer: You can amend your return using Form 1040-X. Taxly will guide you through the amendment process. There's no additional filing fee for amendments.

q3.question: How do I file an extension?
q3.answer: File Form 4868 by April 15 to get a 6-month extension to October 15. Taxly handles this automatically. Note: an extension to file is not an extension to pay — any tax owed is still due April 15.

q4.question: Can I file jointly with my spouse?
q4.answer: Yes. Choose 'Married Filing Jointly' when you start. Taxly will ask for your spouse's information and compare MFJ vs MFS to find the better outcome.

about.heading: Built by people who believe taxes shouldn't be this hard
about.subheading: Taxly is made by the team behind Finovo — accounting software for European small businesses. We saw how broken the US tax filing experience was and decided to fix it.

mission.heading: Our mission
mission.text:
The average American spends 13 hours filing their taxes. That's a full workday lost to paperwork, jargon, and anxiety — every year. We think that's wrong.

Taxly exists to give those hours back. Guided questions instead of confusing forms. Flat prices instead of bait-and-switch fees. Plain English instead of IRS jargon.

We're not trying to replace your accountant. We're trying to make sure you don't need one for a simple return.

team.heading: Who builds Taxly
team.text:
We're AppsLab — a small, compliance-first software company. Finovo, our European accounting product, is used by thousands of freelancers and small businesses in the UK, France, and Germany.

Taxly brings the same engineering rigour and obsessive attention to accuracy to the US market.

contact.heading: Get in touch
contact.subheading: Questions, feedback, or press enquiries — we read everything.

form.name: Your name
form.subject: Subject
form.success: Message sent. We'll get back to you within one business day.
form.error: Something went wrong. Please try again or email us directly.

privacy.heading: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: Taxly Inc. ("Taxly", "we", "us", "our") is committed to protecting your privacy. This policy explains what information we collect, how we use it, and your rights.

collect.heading: Information we collect
collect.text: We collect information you provide directly: your name, email address, Social Security number, income information, and other tax-related data necessary to prepare your return. We also collect usage data (pages visited, features used) to improve the product.

use.heading: How we use your information
use.text: We use your information solely to prepare, review, and e-file your tax return; to send you account and filing status notifications; and to improve Taxly's accuracy and user experience. We do not use your tax data for advertising.

share.heading: Information sharing
share.text: We share your tax information with the IRS and relevant state tax authorities solely for the purpose of filing your return, as authorized by you. We do not sell your information to third parties. We may share aggregate, non-identifiable usage data with service providers who help us operate Taxly.

security.heading: Security
security.text: We protect your data with AES-256 encryption at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit, and strict access controls. See our Security page for full details.

retention.heading: Data retention
retention.text: We retain your filed returns and associated data for 7 years to comply with IRS record-keeping guidance and to allow you to access prior returns. You can request deletion of your account at any time; we will delete your data within 30 days, except where retention is required by law.

rights.heading: Your rights
rights.text: You may access, correct, or delete your personal information at any time through Account Settings. To request full data deletion, email privacy@gettaxly.com. We do not discriminate against users who exercise these rights.

terms.heading: Terms of Service
terms.intro: These Terms of Service govern your use of Taxly. By using Taxly you agree to these terms.

service.heading: The service
service.text: Taxly provides tax preparation software that helps you prepare and e-file US federal and state income tax returns. Taxly is not a CPA firm, law firm, or licensed tax preparer. We provide software tools, not professional tax advice.

accuracy.heading: Your responsibility for accuracy
accuracy.text: You are responsible for the accuracy and completeness of all information you enter into Taxly. Taxly calculates your return based on the information you provide. Errors or omissions in your input are not covered by our Accuracy Guarantee.

efile.heading: Electronic filing consent
efile.text: By submitting your return for e-filing, you authorize Taxly to transmit your return to the IRS and applicable state tax authorities on your behalf. You confirm that the return is true, correct, and complete to the best of your knowledge, as required by IRS Publication 1345.

payment.heading: Fees and payment
payment.text: Fees are charged at the time of e-filing. Prices are displayed before you pay. We do not charge hidden fees. Refunds are available if you have paid but not yet e-filed, within 30 days of payment.

prohibited.heading: Prohibited uses
prohibited.text: You may not use Taxly to file returns on behalf of others without their explicit authorization, to file fraudulent or knowingly inaccurate returns, or to attempt to access other users' accounts or data.

liability.heading: Limitation of liability
liability.text: To the maximum extent permitted by law, Taxly's liability for any claim arising from your use of the service is limited to the amount you paid for the filing in question. We are not liable for IRS decisions, state tax authority decisions, or penalties arising from information you entered incorrectly.

changes.heading: Changes to these terms
changes.text: We may update these terms. We'll notify you by email and post the updated terms at gettaxly.com/terms at least 14 days before changes take effect.

q1.question: Is Taxly really free for simple returns?
q1.answer: Yes. Federal filing is free for W-2 income with the standard deduction, no income limit. One state return is also free.

q2.question: When is the tax deadline?
q2.answer: The federal deadline is April 15 for most filers. If you need more time, you can file a free extension to October 15 — Taxly handles that too.

q3.question: Is my data secure?
q3.answer: All data is encrypted with AES-256 at rest and TLS 1.3 in transit. We never sell your data or use it for advertising.

q4.question: Can I import from last year's return?
q4.answer: Yes. Import a PDF from TurboTax, H&R Block, or any prior Taxly return and we'll pre-fill your name, address, and prior-year figures.

q5.question: What if I have both W-2 and freelance income?
q5.answer: Start with Premium. It handles W-2 plus Schedule C (self-employment) in the same return, including self-employment tax calculation.

q6.question: How fast is my refund?
q6.answer: The IRS typically deposits refunds within 21 days of acceptance for e-filed returns. State refunds vary by state.

cookiesPage.heading: Cookie Policy
cookiesPage.intro: This page lists every cookie and similar technology gettaxly.com uses, what it does, and how long it lasts. Analytics cookies load only after you accept them in the consent banner — declining keeps the site fully functional.
cookiesPage.consentNote: Your consent choice itself is stored in your browser's localStorage (not a cookie) under the key gf_cookie_consent, so we can remember your decision without tracking you.

rows.name: gf_cookie_consent
rows.purpose: Remembers whether you accepted or declined analytics

rows.name: _ga / _ga_*
rows.purpose: Distinguishes visitors and sessions for aggregate usage statistics

rows.name: _clck / _clsk
rows.purpose: Session replay and heatmaps to improve the site

rows.name: Vercel Analytics
rows.purpose: Cookieless, aggregated page-view counts (no identifier stored on your device)

cookiesPage.managing: You can withdraw consent at any time by clearing your browser data for this site — the banner will ask again on your next visit. Blocking all cookies in your browser also works; nothing on this site breaks without them.

subProcessorsPage.heading: Sub-processors
subProcessorsPage.intro: These are the third-party services that process data on our behalf when you use gettaxly.com. We list them by name because you can't judge our privacy promises without knowing who we share data with.

hub.heading: Free tax tools — no signup, no catch
hub.intro: Three calculators that answer the questions people actually search for. Every result is an illustrative estimate with the assumptions stated, and every rate links to the IRS source so you can verify it.

refund.title: Refund estimator
refund.text: A rough TY2026 federal refund or balance-due estimate from your income, withholding, and household.

penalty.title: Penalty estimator
penalty.text: Missed the April deadline? Estimate failure-to-file and failure-to-pay penalties plus interest — and what to do right now.

quarterly.title: Quarterly tax calculator
quarterly.text: Freelance or gig income? Work out your safe-harbor quarterly payments and see the self-employment tax before it surprises you.

hub.note: These tools never ask for your name, email, or SSN. They run in your browser and exist because good answers to scary questions are the most honest marketing we know.

penalty.heading: IRS penalty estimator
penalty.intro: Missed the deadline with tax owed? Get an honest estimate of the failure-to-file penalty, the failure-to-pay penalty, and interest — and the moves that stop the bleeding.
penalty.fileFirstNote: The single most important fact: the penalty for not FILING is ten times the penalty for not PAYING. If you haven't filed, file now — even if you can't pay a dollar of it yet.
penalty.taxHint: The unpaid balance from your return (not your total tax).
penalty.monthsHint: Part of a month counts as a full month — that's the IRS rule, not ours.
penalty.filedLabel: I filed my return (or an extension) on time
penalty.filedHint: Filing on time removes the big 5%/month penalty; only late payment and interest remain.
penalty.errorBlank: Enter both amounts — a blank field isn't zero.
penalty.total: Estimated total on top of your tax
penalty.minimumNote: Your return is over 60 days late, so the minimum failure-to-file penalty applied — the lesser of the fixed floor or 100% of the unpaid tax.

advice.heading: What to do right now
advice.file: 1. File the return today if you haven't — that stops the 5%/month penalty immediately.
advice.plan: 2. Can't pay in full? The IRS offers payment plans; being on one cuts the failure-to-pay rate in half.
advice.abatement: 3. First time this happened? Ask the IRS about first-time penalty abatement — many filers qualify and never ask.
advice.cta: Taxly opens for TY2026 — join the waitlist

penalty.disclaimer: Illustrative estimate, not tax advice. Rates and the minimum-penalty floor change; verify current figures at irs.gov/payments/penalties before acting.

quarterly.heading: Quarterly estimated tax calculator
quarterly.intro: No employer withholds for freelance income. This works out the safe-harbor payments that keep the IRS penalty away — from the one number you already know: last year's tax.
quarterly.priorHint: The “total tax” line on last year's Form 1040 — not your refund or payment.
quarterly.withholdingHint: If you also have a day job, its withholding counts toward the target. No job? Leave it at 0.
quarterly.highAgiHint: High earners must hit 110% of last year's tax instead of 100%.
quarterly.profitHint: Income minus expenses. Adding it shows the self-employment tax most first-year freelancers don't see coming.
quarterly.errorBlank: Enter last year's tax and your withholding — a blank field isn't zero.
quarterly.resultNote: Targets {pct} of last year's tax ({target}). Pay this through the year and there's no underpayment penalty, whatever this year's final bill turns out to be.
quarterly.dueDates: Due roughly April 15, June 15, September 15, and January 15 — verify each year's exact dates at irs.gov.
quarterly.seHeading: Heads up: self-employment tax on that profit
quarterly.seNote: 15.3% Social Security and Medicare on 92.35% of profit — on top of income tax, because no employer is paying half of it for you. (Approximation: the Social Security portion caps at the annual wage base for high profits.)

cta.text: Taxly's Premium tier is built around exactly this return — 1099 income, deductions found, quarterly reminders.

quarterly.disclaimer: Illustrative estimate, not tax advice. Safe-harbor rules and dates come from IRS Form 1040-ES — verify the current year's figures at irs.gov.
